title: gh#68: R3.3 — Switch worker/token-refresh-worker to TokenVault
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/68.
Backlog section: Candidate 3 — TokenVault.

## Context

Third PR in Candidate 3. The token-refresh-worker is the only callsite that uses *both* unseal and seal directions, so it's the most cipher-heavy file. Migration is mechanical: every `decrypt`/`encrypt` call becomes a vault method.

## Scope

- Modify `packages/worker/src/token-refresh-worker.ts`:
  - Inject `vault: TokenVault` via the worker factory (similar to how `notificationQueue` is injected today)
  - Replace `decrypt(refreshCt, refreshIv, refreshTag, encryptionKey)` (line ~269) with `vault.unsealOAuth2RefreshToken(profile)`
  - Replace `decrypt(accessCt, accessIv, accessTag, encryptionKey)` (line ~328) with `vault.unsealForProfile(profile)` and narrow to the OAuth 2.0 variant
  - Replace `encrypt(response.access_token, encryptionKey)` (line ~289) with `vault.sealOAuth2AccessToken(response.access_token)`
  - Replace any refresh-token re-encrypt with `vault.sealOAuth2RefreshToken(...)` 
  - Remove `process.env.ENCRYPTION_KEY` reads and `validateEncryptionKey` calls inside the worker — vault is injected
- Update `packages/worker/src/index.ts` `main()` to construct the vault once via `createTokenVault(validateEncryptionKey(rawKey))` and pass it to the token-refresh-worker factory
- Update token-refresh-worker tests to inject a fake vault

## Out of scope

- twitter-delete (R3.4)
- Publishers (R3.5)

## Acceptance

- `pnpm --filter @sms/worker build` and test pass
- `rg "decrypt\(|encrypt\(" packages/worker/src/token-refresh-worker.ts` returns no matches
- `rg "process\.env\.ENCRYPTION_KEY" packages/worker/src/token-refresh-worker.ts` returns no matches
- Existing token-refresh test coverage maintained

## References

- Depends on: #66
